title: Fix Stored Packet Count Parsing from PktState Column

requirements.summary: Fix CSV parser to correctly extract Stored packet count from the PktState column by counting rows where PktState equals 'Stored', and update the import section to submit the parsed storedPkts value to the backend
acceptanceCriteria: When a file is uploaded with a 'PktState' column containing 'Normal' and 'Stored' values, the parser correctly counts rows where PktState is 'Stored' and assigns that value to storedPkts.
The STORED PKTS column in the Weekly Reports table shows non-zero values matching the actual count of 'Stored' state packets from the uploaded file.
Normal + Stored packet counts sum to equal the Total packets hit count.
Existing Normal packet count parsing remains correct and unaffected.

file_operations.description: Update the parseCSV function to count rows where PktState column equals 'Stored' (case-insensitive) and assign that count to the storedPkts field in the parsed output. Currently storedPkts is always set to totalPkts (which is incorrect). The correct logic is: count rows where row[pktStateCol] === 'Stored' and set that as storedPkts. Ensure normalPkts continues to count rows where PktState is 'Normal', and that totalPkts represents the total row count (Normal + Stored).

Update the handleImport function to pass the parsed storedPkts value (in addition to the existing normalPkts) when calling upsertReport. Currently the function may not be passing storedPkts correctly to the backend. Verify that both storedPkts and normalPkts are included in the upsertReport call parameters and match the backend interface signature (unit, id, week, total, stored, valid, storedPkts, normalPkts).
